data.title: A whale is suspected of clearing 1,290 ETH and making a profit of $950,000
data.description: On-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa monitored that the whale who built an ETH position at $1,768 on April 25th seemed to have liquidated 1,290 ETH (about $3.23 million) half an hour ago, and would have made a profit of $950,000 if sold.

data.title: Stocks and Crypto Tick Downward As Trump Accuses China of Violating Trade Agreement
data.description:
Stocks and crypto lost a little bit of ground on Friday as President Donald Trump accused China of violating a recent trade agreement. Trump says on Truth Social that he had previously decided to make a deal with China because his tariffs were plunging the Asian giant into “grave economic danger.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/05/30/stocks-and-crypto-tick-downward-as-trump-accuses-china-of-violating-trade-agreement/

data.title: A whale's 25x leveraged long ETH position increased to $75.27 million, with a floating loss of $1.4 million
data.description: Onchain Lens monitoring shows that a whale deposited 1.7 million USDC (a total of 4.3 million US dollars) into HyperLiquid, increasing its ETH long position with 25 times leverage. Currently, the nominal value of the ETH position has reached 75.27 million US dollars, with a floating loss of 1.4 million US dollars.

data.title: Meta shareholders voted down proposal to add Bitcoin to reserves
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: At the annual general meeting of shareholders on May 30, Meta Platforms shareholders rejected a proposal to add Bitcoin (BTC) to its $72 billion cash reserves. The vote ended with 4.98 billion shares against and 3.92 million shares in favor. (CryptoSlate)

data.title: The U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF had a net outflow of $614.02 million yesterday, the largest single-day outflow since February 26
data.description: Trader T monitoring shows that the U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF had a net outflow of US$614.02 million yesterday, the largest single-day outflow since February 26.

data.title: A whale missed out on a $10 million profit and lost $2.47 million by short selling BTC. He then spent 43.36 million USDT to buy ETH and is currently losing $1.66 million.
data.description: According to the on-chain analyst Ember Monitoring, a whale bought BTC at the bottom of $85,751 at the end of March. If it got it, it was expected to make a profit of more than $10 million. However, in mid-April, the whale sold it at $84,426. A few days later, BTC quickly rose to $110,000. After selling the spot, the whale borrowed 124 WBTC and sold it short at $84,558. The 124 WBTC sold short were bought back at $104,444 10 hours ago, with a loss of $2.47 million. After the repayment, the whale bought 16,638 ETH with 43.36 million USDT, with an average purchase price of $2,606. The current ETH price is about $100 lower than its purchase price, with a floating loss of $1.66 million.

data.title: Meta Platforms Shareholders Reject Bitcoin Financial Assessment Proposal
data.description: According to Foresight News, Phoenix reports that Meta Platforms shareholders have voted against a proposal for a financial assessment of Bitcoin. The decision reflects the company's stance on cryptocurrency evaluations and its approach to digital asset management. Shareholders expressed concerns over the implications of such an assessment on Meta's financial strategy and future investments. The rejection highlights ongoing debates within the corporate sector regarding the integration of cryptocurrencies into traditional financial systems.

data.title: Top Trimmed, Risk Managed – Outperformance Through Tactical Cuts
data.description:
Our BTC overweight provided stability as markets turned choppy, while timely trims in ETH and SOL helped us sidestep the worst of the altcoin pullback. By raising cash ahead of the weekend, we avoided the selloff and outperformed our benchmark through disciplined, defensive positioning.

data.title: Bitcoin mining difficulty increased by 4.38% to 126.98 T, a record high
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News BTC.com data shows that the difficulty of Bitcoin mining has been adjusted at block height 899,136 (Beijing time today 08:01:30), and the mining difficulty has been increased by 4.38% to 126.98 T, a record high; the average computing power of the entire network in the past seven days is 921.04 EH/s.

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Difficulty Reaches Record High with 4.38% Increase
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from BTC.com indicates that Bitcoin mining difficulty has been adjusted at block height 899,136, with an increase of 4.38% to reach 126.98 T, marking a new historical high. As of now, the average network hash rate over the past seven days stands at 921.04 EH/s.

Ethereum(ETH) Drops Below 2,500 USDT with a 4.16% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On May 31, 2025, 01:35 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 2,500 USDT and is now trading at 2,488.959961 USDT, with a narrowed 4.16% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Declines to 16, Reflecting Market Trends
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Coinmarketcap indicates that the Altcoin Season Index is currently at 16, a decrease from its peak of 43 on May 10. This index reveals that in the past 90 days, approximately 16 out of the top 100 cryptocurrencies by market capitalization have outperformed Bitcoin.

The CMC Altcoin Season Index serves as a real-time indicator to assess whether the cryptocurrency market is experiencing a period dominated by altcoins. It evaluates the performance of the top 100 altcoins relative to Bitcoin over the last 90 days.
